name: chntpw
title: chntpw
description:
NT SAM password recovery utility
This little program provides a way to view information and
change user passwords in a Windows NT/2000 user database file.
Old passwords need not be known since they are overwritten.
In addition it also contains a simple registry editor
(same size data writes) and an hex-editor which enables you to
fiddle around with bits and bytes in the file as you wish.
.
If you want GNU/Linux bootdisks for offline password recovery
you can add this utility to custom image disks or use those provided
at the tools homepage.

commands.name: reged
commands.description: utility to export/import and edit a Windows registry hives

commands.name: sampasswd
commands.description: reset passwords of users in the SAM user database

commands.name: samunlock
commands.description: unlock users in the SAM user database

commands.name: samusrgrp
commands.description: add or remove users from groups in SAM database files

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use chntpw because NT SAM password recovery utility.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use chntpw as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `chntpw [options] <samfile> [systemfile] [securityfile] [otherreghive] [...]` as documented upstream for chntpw.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `chntpw SAM system` as documented upstream for chntpw.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `chntpw -l SAM` as documented upstream for chntpw.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `chntpw -u jabbathehutt SAM` as documented upstream for chntpw.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair chntpw with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `chntpw --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: chntpw - utility to overwrite passwords of Windows systems
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install chntpw. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `chntpw [options] <samfile> [systemfile] [securityfile] [otherreghive] [...]`. Further invocations from upstream: `chntpw -l SAM`; `chntpw -u jabbathehutt SAM`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: NT SAM password recovery utility.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: chntpw - utility to overwrite passwords of Windows systems.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install chntpw
